perturbation.original_text: 1.1 APPOINTMENT AS AGENT. NCB appoints NPC, and NPC agrees to serve, as NCB's sole agent (i) to provide authorization, processing and settlement services with respect to Visa and MasterCard transactions ("Merchant Processing Services") to merchants who desire to receive Merchant Processing Services from NCB or NPC ("Merchants") and (ii) to enter into contracts with merchants ("Merchant Contracts") for the provision of Merchant Processing Services as agent of NCB; provided, however, that nothing herein shall limit NPC's right to provide, as agent for other members of Visa and MasterCard, Merchant Processing Services to merchants who desire to receive such services from NCB or others. NCB agrees that NPC may use NCB's name and its BIN, ICA and any other Visa and MasterCard identification numbers to the extent necessary or appropriate to perform the Merchant Processing Services.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 APPOINTMENT AS AGENT. NCB appoints NPC, and NPC agrees to serve, as NCB's sole representative (i) to provide authorization, processing and settlement services with respect to Visa and MasterCard transactions ("Merchant Processing Services") to merchants who desire to receive Merchant Processing Services from NCB or NPC ("Merchants") and (ii) to enter into contracts with merchants ("Merchant Contracts") for the provision of Merchant Processing Services as representative of NCB; provided, however, that nothing herein shall limit NPC's right to provide, as representative for other members of Visa and MasterCard, Merchant Processing Services to merchants who desire to receive such services from NCB or others. NCB agrees that NPC may use NCB's name and its BIN, ICA and any other Visa and MasterCard identification numbers to the extent necessary or appropriate to perform the Merchant Processing Services.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation changes 'agent' to 'representative.' While seemingly synonymous, legal distinctions exist. Throughout the original contract, 'agent' implies specific legal duties and liabilities. Switching to 'representative' introduces ambiguity as this term isn't clearly defined within the contract. The rest of the document uses agent, creating an in-text contradiction and uncertainty about the true scope of NPC's authority and liability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.3 MERCHANT CONTRACTS. NPC, as agent of NCB, shall be responsible for establishing the terms and conditions of the Merchant Contracts, including all changes thereunder, subject to Section 1.7 below. Without in any way limiting the authority granted in Section 1.1, NCB hereby authorizes NPC to use NCB's name to the extent necessary or appropriate in accordance with the terms of this Agreement to enter into and to renew Merchant Contracts, to modify Merchant Contracts to the extent necessary to assign them to NCB, to perform the Merchant Contracts and to take other necessary or appropriate actions with respect to the Merchant Contracts, all in accordance with requirements of Visa and MasterCard. As between NPC and NCB, NPC shall be responsible for all credit, fraud and other risks associated with each Merchant Contract.
perturbation.justification: The modification replaces 'agent' with 'representative,' introducing ambiguity regarding NPC's authority. The contradicted text (1.3) explicitly states "NPC, as agent of NCB, shall be responsible..." and assigns liabilities based on this agency relationship. By changing 'agent' to 'representative' in 1.1, the contract creates uncertainty about whether NPC still bears those same responsibilities outlined in 1.3, as the scope and legal implications of 'representative' are not defined, thus creating a direct conflict regarding the scope of NPC's liability and obligations as an agent, making the obligations and liabilities of the changed definition unclear and placing the text in direct contradiction with the original intention of assigning duties to the 'agent'.

perturbation.original_text: 1.5 PROCESSING AND SUBMISSION TO INTERCHANGE. NPC shall process all data received by it reflecting the Visa and MasterCard sales transactions and any related return credits by the Merchants and shall submit to the applicable Visa or MasterCard interchange networks ("Interchange") "Settlement Files" reflecting such transactions and directing the applicable Interchange to pay the net amount due to an account established by NCB for the purpose of receiving all settlement amounts paid by Visa and MasterCard with respect to the transactions processed by NPC pursuant to this Agreement and effecting appropriate payments to the Merchants and NPC in accordance with this Agreement ("the NCB Account").
perturbation.changed_text: 1.5 PROCESSING AND SUBMISSION TO INTERCHANGE. NPC shall process all data received by it reflecting the Visa and MasterCard sales transactions and any related return credits by the Merchants and shall submit to the applicable Visa or MasterCard interchange networks ("Interchange") "Transaction Summaries" reflecting such transactions and directing the applicable Interchange to pay the net amount due to an account established by NCB for the purpose of receiving all settlement amounts paid by Visa and MasterCard with respect to the transactions processed by NPC pursuant to this Agreement and effecting appropriate payments to the Merchants and NPC in accordance with this Agreement ("the NCB Account").
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation replaces 'Settlement Files' with 'Transaction Summaries.' The term Settlement Files is defined in this section, but Transaction Summaries is not. This lack of definition introduces ambiguity. The contract refers to 'Settlement Files' elsewhere, creating an in-text contradiction regarding what NPC is obligated to submit to Interchange. The term summary can have different meanings and creates an ambiguity, opening the contract for misinterpretation and increasing the potential for disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.6 ACCOUNT SETTLEMENT. (a) PAYMENT INSTRUCTIONS. NPC shall prepare and transmit to NCB, in a mutually acceptable format, instructions specifying the payments to be made from the NCB Account to the Merchants and to NPC pursuant to this Agreement (the "Payment Instructions"). Payment Instructions shall be delivered to NCB in accordance with the schedule and procedures established from time to time by the parties. (b) PAYMENT. In accordance with the Payment Instructions, NCB shall pay the Merchants on a timely basis the net settlement amounts due to them and shall credit NPC's account at NCB for the fees due to NPC hereunder. Such payments to Merchants shall be effected through mutually acceptable procedures which are consistent with the payment procedures established pursuant to the Merchant Contracts. NPC shall perform on NCB's behalf all of the administrative and bookkeeping functions necessary to effect payment in such manner.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Settlement Files' with 'Transaction Summaries.' The original text defines 'Settlement Files', and the contradicted text in 1.6 (a) references 'Payment Instructions', which are based on the information from 'Settlement Files'. Because 'Transaction Summaries' is undefined, and 'Settlement Files' is what the contradicted text expects and relies on, this change creates a direct contradiction. This introduces ambiguity and makes it unclear what information NPC must send to NCB to generate the Payment Instructions.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1 INDEMNIFICATION. NPC agrees to indemnify, defend and save NCB, its directors, officers and employees harmless from all losses, claims, judgments, awards, penalties, expenses and other amounts of any nature arising out of: (a) NPC's failure to perform this Agreement in accordance with its terms, including, but not limited to, the failure to pay expenses, charges and other amounts in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement, 4 5 (b) The negligent exercise of or the exceeding by any Joint Officer of the authority granted pursuant to this Agreement to act as an officer of NCB, or, (c) NCB's grant of authority to NPC pursuant to this Agreement, including, but not limited to, all court costs, investigation expenses and the reasonable fees and expenses of separate counsel for NCB selected by NCB, provided, however, that NCB shall not be entitled to indemnification as to amounts arising from the negligence or willful misconduct of NCB.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1 INDEMNIFICATION. NPC agrees to indemnify, defend and save NCB, its directors, officers and staff harmless from all losses, claims, judgments, awards, penalties, expenses and other amounts of any nature arising out of: (a) NPC's failure to perform this Agreement in accordance with its terms, including, but not limited to, the failure to pay expenses, charges and other amounts in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement, 4 5 (b) The negligent exercise of or the exceeding by any Joint Officer of the authority granted pursuant to this Agreement to act as an officer of NCB, or, (c) NCB's grant of authority to NPC pursuant to this Agreement, including, but not limited to, all court costs, investigation expenses and the reasonable fees and expenses of separate counsel for NCB selected by NCB, provided, however, that NCB shall not be entitled to indemnification as to amounts arising from the negligence or willful misconduct of NCB.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation changes 'employees' to 'staff.' While often used interchangeably, there is a subtle legal difference. "Employees" generally implies a formal employer-employee relationship with associated legal obligations. "Staff" is a broader term that could encompass contractors, consultants, or even volunteers. Throughout the rest of the document the word Employees is used. This creates an in-text contradiction. It raises questions about whether NCB is protected from liabilities caused by individuals who are "staff" but not technically "employees," which directly affects the scope and effectiveness of the indemnification clause. This change undermines the clarity and enforceability of the indemnification provision.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.6 MONITORING BY NCB. NPC shall admit properly identified and authorized NCB employees and agents onto its premises for purposes of monitoring NPC's compliance with this Agreement. It is understood that such monitoring will occur during normal business hours, will be preceded by reasonable notification to NPC, and must not interfere with NPC's normal operations.
perturbation.justification: The modification changes 'employees' to 'staff' in the indemnification clause (5.1). This creates an in-text contradiction with section 10.6, which specifically refers to 'NCB employees'. By broadening the term to 'staff' in the indemnification clause, it introduces ambiguity as to whether NCB is protected from liabilities caused by all individuals considered 'staff', even if they are not technically 'employees'. This undermines the clarity and enforceability of the indemnification provision as it's unclear if monitoring (10.6) applies to 'staff' in the same way it applies to 'employees' and thus whether liabilities arising from 'staff' are indemnified under 5.1. The definition of Misaligned Terminology fits this scenario.
